classDescription: The broadest spell list in the game, backed by Intelligence-based preparation and ritual casting — a flexible full caster who shapes fights through utility, control, and access to answers most classes can't reach.
roleIdentity: Highly flexible full caster with best ritual and utility breadth.

wizard-spellcasting.summary: Maintain a spellbook, prepare arcane tools from it, and solve problems through the broadest magical list in the game.
wizard-spellcasting.description:
Wizard spellcasting is about breadth, planning, and precision. You maintain a spellbook, prepare a subset of spells for the day, and then answer encounters with exactly the right arcane tool when you read the situation well.

The sheet should make this feel distinct from spontaneous casters: a wizard is rewarded for knowledge, preparation, and using the spellbook as a real resource.

wizard-arcane-recovery.summary: Restore part of your spell economy during a short rest so the wizard can stay useful across a full adventuring day.
wizard-arcane-recovery.description: Arcane Recovery helps the wizard avoid spending all meaningful spell power in the first major encounter. It should be surfaced as a rest-phase decision that rewards smart slot management rather than panic casting.
wizard-arcane-recovery.recovery: You can use this once per day after finishing a Short Rest.

wizard-scholar.summary: Your training is not just magical talent — it is practiced, studied expertise in arcane knowledge and careful problem solving.
wizard-scholar.description: Scholar is the wizard identity reminder that knowledge matters. It should communicate that this class solves scenes through preparation, research, recall, and careful arcane understanding, not only damage output.

wizard-arcane-tradition-subclass.summary: Your chosen school or specialty changes the shape of your wizardry and the kinds of magical advantages you build around.
wizard-arcane-tradition-subclass.description: Arcane Tradition is the subclass gate for wizard. This is where the class stops being a generic spellcaster and becomes an Abjurer, Diviner, Evoker, or Illusionist with a focused play pattern and a reason to approach encounters differently.

wizard-memorize-spell.summary: Reconfigure part of your prepared toolkit more flexibly than most casters, keeping the wizard adaptable between problems.
wizard-memorize-spell.description: Memorize Spell reinforces the wizard fantasy of careful preparation and mid-adventure adaptation. The point is not raw power; it is the ability to pivot when new information changes which spell will matter next.

wizard-cantrip-formulas.summary: Your basic magical routines become easier to swap and refine so your fallback toolkit stays useful.
wizard-cantrip-formulas.description: Cantrip Formulas matters because cantrips are used constantly. This feature should feel like wizard flexibility scaled down to the at-will level: a small but meaningful quality-of-life advantage that makes daily preparation less rigid.

wizard-spell-mastery.summary: Choose signature lower-level spells that become part of your permanent operating rhythm instead of your precious daily budget.
wizard-spell-mastery.description: Spell Mastery is one of the clearest high-level wizard payoffs. It turns a couple of dependable spells into tools you can lean on repeatedly, freeing your main slots for more dramatic effects and making the class feel truly seasoned.

wizard-signature-spells.summary: Your most iconic prepared spells become effortless enough that they define how this wizard approaches every serious adventuring day.
wizard-signature-spells.description: Signature Spells is the capstone-level expression of wizard mastery. By this point, the character has favorite spells that are woven so deeply into their routine that they feel almost reflexive, and the sheet should present that as a real class identity payoff.

wizard-spell-level-2.summary: You can now prepare and cast 2nd-level wizard spells.
wizard-spell-level-2.description: Second-level spells open broad utility and combat control options that markedly expand tactical planning.

wizard-subclass-feature-6.summary: Your Arcane Tradition grants its first advanced specialization feature.
wizard-subclass-feature-6.description: At level 6, subclass identity should begin to materially change your encounter decisions and spell priorities.

wizard-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Increase Intelligence or take a feat to sharpen your arcane toolkit.
wizard-ability-score-improvement-4.description: This ASI often determines whether a wizard focuses on higher save DCs, survivability, or utility feat support.

wizard-spell-level-3.summary: You gain access to 3rd-level wizard spells and major battlefield swing options.
wizard-spell-level-3.description: Third-level magic is a major breakpoint for control, defense, and encounter-shaping spells.

wizard-cantrips-known-4.summary: Learn a fourth cantrip to broaden your at-will magical coverage.
wizard-cantrips-known-4.description: Additional cantrips reduce dead turns and increase flexibility when conserving spell slots.

wizard-subclass-feature-10.summary: Your Arcane Tradition deepens with a high-impact specialist feature.
wizard-subclass-feature-10.description: This level should feel like a real archetype advancement, not just a ribbon.

wizard-spell-level-4.summary: You can now cast 4th-level wizard spells.
wizard-spell-level-4.description: Fourth-level spells provide stronger control and mobility options that significantly expand problem-solving patterns.

wizard-ability-score-improvement-8.summary: Another ASI/feat choice to improve spell reliability or durability.
wizard-ability-score-improvement-8.description: This mid-tier ASI is often used to push Intelligence toward cap or secure concentration-support feats.

wizard-spell-level-5.summary: You gain 5th-level spell access and premier midgame arcane effects.
wizard-spell-level-5.description: Fifth-level spells often define encounter pacing and strategic leverage for wizard-focused parties.

wizard-spell-level-6.summary: You can cast 6th-level spells, entering high-tier arcane play.
wizard-spell-level-6.description: Sixth-level magic introduces powerful single-cast answers that can reshape hard encounters.

wizard-ability-score-improvement-12.summary: Late-tier ASI/feat choice for endgame spellcasting stability.
wizard-ability-score-improvement-12.description: At level 12 this choice often strengthens concentration resilience, initiative control, or maximized Intelligence.

wizard-subclass-feature-14.summary: Your Arcane Tradition unlocks its capstone specialization feature.
wizard-subclass-feature-14.description: This is the defining late-tier subclass payoff and should be clearly presented in the feature catalog.

wizard-spell-level-7.summary: You can now cast 7th-level wizard spells.
wizard-spell-level-7.description: Seventh-level spell access introduces encounter-defining arcane options with major strategic impact.

wizard-spell-level-8.summary: You gain access to 8th-level wizard spells.
wizard-spell-level-8.description: Eighth-level spells provide exceptional control and reality-bending utility suited to late-campaign play.

wizard-ability-score-improvement-16.summary: Penultimate ASI/feat to finish your endgame wizard build.
wizard-ability-score-improvement-16.description: Level 16 choices typically lock in final survivability or optimization priorities before top-tier spell access.

wizard-spell-level-9.summary: You can cast 9th-level wizard spells, the peak of arcane power.
wizard-spell-level-9.description: Ninth-level spells are campaign-shaping resources and should be framed as pinnacle magical capabilities.

wizard-cantrips-known-5.summary: Learn a fifth cantrip for broader at-will utility and damage profiles.
wizard-cantrips-known-5.description: This helps the wizard maintain flexible no-slot options in long adventuring days.

wizard-epic-boon.summary: Gain an Epic Boon for a mythic-tier post-cap progression reward.
wizard-epic-boon.description: Epic Boons represent endgame personalization beyond standard wizard class scaling.

progressionSummary.summary: Subclass Feature, Cantrip Formulas, Cantrips Known: 5.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your spell attacks and save DC
buildTips.secondaryReason: helps with HP and Concentration on critical spells
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: wizards are fragile, so AC and Dexterity saves matter a lot
